public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize a 3-phase inverter PWM using the unified eGTM PWM driver on eGTM Cluster 1 ATOM1 with three logical channels (0, 1, 2). The function performs the following steps in order: 1) Declare all local configuration structures: IfxEgtm_Pwm_Config config; arrays IfxEgtm_Pwm_OutputConfig output[3]; IfxEgtm_Pwm_DtmConfig dtmConfig[3]; IfxEgtm_Pwm_InterruptConfig interruptConfig; and IfxEgtm_Pwm_ChannelConfig channelConfig[3]. Also use a persistent module-state structure (e.g., g_egtmAtom3phInv) that contains: the IfxEgtm_Pwm handle, a persistent IfxEgtm_Pwm_Channel channels[3] array (must be persistent because the driver stores its pointer), and arrays for dutyCycles[3], phases[3], and deadTimes[3]. 2) Call IfxEgtm_Pwm_initConfig(&config, &MODULE_EGTM) to load default configuration values. 3) Configure the three complementary outputs in output[]: for each phase i=0..2, assign output[i].pin to the selected high-side TOUT of ATOM1 logical channel i and output[i].complementaryPin to the corresponding low-side TOUT; set output[i].polarity to active-high and output[i].complementaryPolarity to active-low; set output[i].outputMode to push-pull and choose an automotive-grade pad driver. The concrete TOUT pins are provided via board-specific macros and must be validated against the device PinMap (kept abstract here). 4) Configure dead-time via dtmConfig[]: for all three channels set deadTime.rising = 1e-6f and deadTime.falling = 1e-6f (1.0 microsecond each), enabling hardware dead-time insertion by the DTM. 5) Set up a single period-event interrupt using the driver's interrupt configuration structure: set interruptConfig.mode for period notification, isrProvider to CPU0, priority to 20, periodEvent = IfxEgtm_periodEventFunction, and dutyEvent = NULL_PTR. Assign this interrupt only to the base logical channel: channelConfig[0].interrupt = &interruptConfig, and set channelConfig[1].interrupt and channelConfig[2].interrupt to NULL_PTR. 6) Fill channelConfig[] for the three logical ATOM1 channels using ordinal indices: channelConfig[0].timerCh = IfxEgtm_Pwm_SubModule_Ch_0; channelConfig[1].timerCh = IfxEgtm_Pwm_SubModule_Ch_1; channelConfig[2].timerCh = IfxEgtm_Pwm_SubModule_Ch_2. For each channel set phase = 0.0f; set duty to the required initial duties (e.g., 50% for channel 0 if used as V, 25% for channel 1 as U, and 75% for channel 2 as W, matching the intended U/V/W assignment); set dtm = &dtmConfig[i]; set output = &output[i]; set mscOut = NULL_PTR. 7) Complete the main config: select eGTM Cluster 1 and ATOM1 submodule; set frequency to 20 kHz; choose center alignment; select FXCLK0 for the ATOM clock; enable synchronized start (syncStart = TRUE) and synchronized buffered updates (syncUpdate = TRUE); point config.channelConfig to channelConfig[] and set config.numChannels = 3; ensure the output configuration array is linked for each channel via channelConfig[i].output. 8) Check if eGTM is enabled; if not, enable and configure clocks inside this guard: if (!IfxEgtm_isEnabled(&MODULE_EGTM)) { a) IfxEgtm_enable(&MODULE_EGTM); b) read the module frequency dynamically via float32 moduleHz = IfxEgtm_Cmu_getModuleFrequency(&MODULE_EGTM); c) set the GCLK divider to 1:1 using IfxEgtm_Cmu_setGclkDivider(&MODULE_EGTM, 1, 1) so GCLK equals the module clock; d) set the CLK0 divider to 1:1 using IfxEgtm_Cmu_setEclkDivider(&MODULE_EGTM, IfxEgtm_Cmu_Eclk_0, 1, 1) to provide a base clock; e) enable both FXCLK and CLK0 with IfxEgtm_Cmu_enableClocks(&MODULE_EGTM, <mask enabling FXCLK and CLK0>) so ATOM can run from FXCLK0. 9) Initialize the PWM peripheral with IfxEgtm_Pwm_init(&g_egtmAtom3phInv.pwm, &g_egtmAtom3phInv.channels[0], &config). This applies the initial duty and phase values and sets up complementary outputs and dead-time per channel. 10) Copy the initialized duty and dead-time values from channelConfig[] into g_egtmAtom3phInv.dutyCycles[] and g_egtmAtom3phInv.deadTimes[] for runtime tracking. 11) Configure a status GPIO (e.g., a board LED or debug pin) as a push-pull output after PWM init using the IfxPort API; the specific port/pin is board-dependent and not hardcoded here. Notes: No separate ATOM timer or manual SRC setup is performed; the driver's InterruptConfig handles period notification internally. No explicit startSyncedChannels call is required; syncStart = TRUE starts the channels coherently during init.

Perform a synchronous duty increment on the three configured PWM channels. For each duty value in g_egtmAtom3phInv.dutyCycles[0..2], check if adding the configured step PHASE_DUTY_STEP would reach or exceed 100%; if so, wrap that duty back to 0% before incrementing. Then add PHASE_DUTY_STEP to each of the three phases. After all three have been updated in software, call the unified PWM driver to apply the new duties immediately across the synchronized group so that all channels update coherently.
